title: file-block-item --auto-id cannot read the id pattern from a $ref item schema (reads itemSchema.properties.id.pattern, absent on a $ref fragment)
description: file-block-item.ts --auto-id allocates the next id by reading the id PATTERN from the extracted item subschema's properties.id.pattern (file-block-item.ts:110-119 idProp = itemSchema?.properties?.id; pattern at :112). When the items schema is {"$ref":"#/definitions/<kind>"} (features.schema.json:10), the fragment has NO top-level `properties` (they live in the unresolved definition), so idProp is undefined and :157 throws 'Cannot auto-allocate ID: pattern undefined not parseable'. Distinct fix-surface from FGAP-082 (that is the dry-run VALIDATION fragment bug; this is the AUTO-ID pattern-extraction fragment bug) though same fragment-not-resolved root. Symptom-of-FGAP-082's fragment approach but separable: even with whole-file validation fixed, auto-id must resolve the $ref (or read from the definition) to find the id pattern.

evidence.reference: auto-id reads itemSchema.properties.id.pattern; a $ref fragment has no .properties → 'pattern undefined not parseable'
items {"$ref":"#/definitions/feature"}; the id pattern lives in definitions.feature.properties.id, unreachable from the fragment

impact: --auto-id is unusable for any $ref-item block kind (features, spec-reviews); the orchestrator must pass an explicit id (or use raw appendToBlock). Will recur for any conception/.context block kind that adopts $ref item schemas.
proposed_resolution: Resolve the items $ref against the parent schema's definitions before reading properties.id.pattern (or, with FGAP-084, route allocation through a block-api helper that dereferences). One-line-ish once the fragment is dereferenced. [CLOSED 2026-05-23 — commit 5ecd7bd / TASK-060 / VER-032: resolveBlockItemSchema dereferences the items $ref so id.pattern reads correctly; nextId is $ref-aware.]
